userStories.title: Centralized input sanitization middleware for all edge functions
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a shared input sanitization layer applied to every edge function so that injection attacks (SQL, command, template) are blocked before reaching business logic. Shannon-style tools trace data flows from user input to dangerous sinks — this breaks those chains at the boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Create functions/_shared/sanitize.ts with sanitizeInput(), sanitizeSQL(), sanitizeHTML(), sanitizeCommand() utilities
All edge functions import and apply sanitizeInput() to request body/params before processing
SQL-like patterns (UNION SELECT, DROP TABLE, OR 1=1, etc.) are stripped or rejected with 400
Command injection characters (;, |, &&, $(), backticks) are escaped in any string passed to exec-like operations
Unit tests cover OWASP injection cheat sheet payloads (minimum 30 test vectors)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Defense against Shannon's injection exploitation phase. Shannon traces data flows from input to dangerous operations — this sanitization layer breaks every flow at the boundary.

userStories.title: Audit and enforce parameterized queries across all Supabase calls
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every database query to use parameterized inputs (never string interpolation) so that SQL injection is structurally impossible, even if sanitization is bypassed.
acceptanceCriteria: Grep entire codebase for string-interpolated Supabase queries (template literals in .from(), .rpc(), raw SQL)
Convert all interpolated queries to use Supabase client parameterized methods (.eq(), .in(), .filter(), etc.)
Add ESLint rule or custom lint script that flags raw SQL string concatenation/interpolation
Audit all .rpc() calls to ensure parameters are passed as function arguments, not embedded in SQL
Document findings in a SECURITY_AUDIT.md with before/after for each fix
All existing tests still pass
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon's code analysis identifies parameterized vs interpolated queries. Parameterized queries make injection structurally impossible regardless of input content.

userStories.title: Implement DOMPurify sanitization on all dynamic HTML rendering
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all user-generated or API-returned HTML to be sanitized through DOMPurify before rendering, so that stored and reflected XSS payloads are neutralized. Shannon tests XSS by injecting payloads through browser automation.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure DOMPurify as the standard HTML sanitization library
Audit all uses of dangerouslySetInnerHTML across the codebase and wrap each with DOMPurify.sanitize()
Audit TipTap blog editor output rendering — ensure stored blog HTML is sanitized on display
Audit all locations where API response data is rendered as HTML (AI-generated content, recipe instructions, etc.)
Create a shared SafeHTML component: <SafeHTML html={content} /> that applies DOMPurify automatically
Add unit tests with OWASP XSS cheat sheet payloads (<script>, onerror, javascript:, data:text/html, SVG payloads)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon's XSS agent injects real payloads via browser automation. DOMPurify at every rendering boundary neutralizes stored, reflected, and DOM-based XSS.

userStories.title: Implement strict Content Security Policy without unsafe-inline
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a strict CSP that eliminates unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval so that even if an XSS payload reaches the browser, it cannot execute. This is the critical layer that makes US-035 actionable.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all inline <script> tags with external script files or nonce-based CSP
Replace all inline style= attributes in components with Tailwind classes or CSS modules

Configure CSP header with: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https://api.tryeatpal.com https://functions.tryeatpal.com
Add CSP nonce generation to Cloudflare Pages _headers or _worker.js
Ensure Three.js, Framer Motion, GSAP, and Lottie still work under the new CSP
Add CSP violation reporting endpoint (report-uri or report-to directive)
Test in report-only mode first, then enforce after 1 week with no breakage
npm run build succeeds and all E2E tests pass
userStories.notes: Supersedes US-035. A strict CSP is the strongest browser-side XSS defense. Shannon's XSS payloads become inert even if they reach the DOM.

userStories.title: Implement URL allowlist validation service for all server-side fetches
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all server-side HTTP requests (fetch, axios, etc.) to pass through a URL validation service that blocks requests to internal/private IP ranges, so that SSRF attacks cannot reach internal infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create functions/_shared/url-validator.ts with validateExternalUrl() that rejects: private IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x), IPv6 loopback/link-local, cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), file:// and other non-http(s) schemes
Resolve DNS before validation to prevent DNS rebinding attacks (resolve hostname, check resulting IP is not private)
Apply validateExternalUrl() to: parse-recipe URL fetching, update-blog-image URL fetching, any OpenAI/external API proxy calls, grocery delivery API calls
Add timeout of 10s on all external fetches to prevent slow-loris style SSRF
Unit tests cover: localhost variations (127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, [::1], 0x7f000001), DNS rebinding scenarios, cloud metadata endpoints, non-HTTP schemes
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Builds on US-004. Shannon's SSRF agent attempts to force internal requests through URL manipulation, DNS rebinding, and IP encoding tricks. This blocks all known SSRF bypass techniques.

userStories.title: Implement CSRF protection on all state-changing operations
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want CSRF tokens validated on every state-changing request so that cross-origin form submissions and fetch requests cannot perform actions on behalf of authenticated users.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement CSRF token generation and validation using Supabase session-bound tokens or a double-submit cookie pattern
All POST/PUT/DELETE edge function endpoints validate CSRF token presence and correctness
Frontend automatically attaches CSRF token to all mutating API requests via a shared fetch wrapper or Supabase client interceptor
Token rotation occurs on each session refresh
Requests without valid CSRF token return 403 Forbidden with descriptive error
Unit tests verify: missing token rejected, invalid token rejected, expired token rejected, valid token accepted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent tests for CSRF by attempting cross-origin state-changing requests. CSRF tokens make these attacks impossible.

userStories.title: Harden session management and cookie security

Implement session ID rotation on login (new session ID after successful authentication)
Implement session ID rotation on privilege escalation (e.g., switching to admin views)
Add absolute session timeout (24h) and idle timeout (2h) with configurable values
Invalidate all other sessions when user changes password
Add session listing in user profile so users can see/revoke active sessions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent tests session fixation, cookie theft, and session replay. Proper session management eliminates these vectors.

userStories.title: Implement progressive rate limiting and account lockout on auth endpoints

Implement account-based rate limiting: 5 failed attempts trigger 15-min lockout, 10 trigger 1-hour lockout, 20 trigger 24-hour lockout with email notification
Rate limiting applies to: /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up, /auth/reset-password, /auth/verify-otp, all OAuth callback endpoints
Add Cloudflare rate limiting rules as first line of defense before edge functions
Return generic 'Invalid credentials' message (never reveal if email exists)
Log all failed auth attempts with IP, user-agent, timestamp for security monitoring

userStories.notes: Extends US-036. Shannon handles complex auth flows including 2FA. Progressive rate limiting stops brute-force and credential stuffing regardless of how sophisticated the attack tool is.

userStories.title: Deploy comprehensive security headers via Cloudflare Pages
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all HTTP responses to include defense-in-depth security headers so that the browser enforces multiple protection layers against common web attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update public/_headers (Cloudflare Pages) with all security headers
Headers include: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Headers include: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Headers include: X-Frame-Options: DENY
Headers include: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
Headers include: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=(self)
Headers include: X-XSS-Protection: 0 (disabled in favor of CSP per modern best practice)
Headers include: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
Headers include: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin
Verify headers present on production deployment using securityheaders.com (target A+ rating)

userStories.notes: Security headers are passive defenses that cost nothing at runtime. They prevent framing attacks, MIME sniffing, information leakage, and cross-origin exploitation — all vectors Shannon probes.

userStories.title: Implement security event monitoring and real-time alerting

acceptanceCriteria: Create a security_events table in Supabase to log: failed auth attempts, rate limit triggers, WAF blocks, CSRF validation failures, input sanitization rejections, suspicious user-agent patterns, unusual API usage patterns
Each event includes: timestamp, IP address, user_id (if authenticated), event_type, severity, request_path, request_method, user_agent, geo_location

Implement real-time alerting thresholds: >10 failed auths from same IP in 5 minutes → alert, >5 injection attempts from same IP in 1 minute → alert + auto-block, >3 SSRF attempts from any IP → alert + auto-block
Alert delivery via: Sentry (error tracking), email notification to security team, Slack webhook (if configured)
Add admin dashboard widget showing security events timeline and top blocked IPs
Implement auto-block: IPs triggering CRITICAL alerts are added to Cloudflare blocklist via API for 24 hours
Retain security events for 90 days minimum
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon runs for 1-1.5 hours generating hundreds of attack requests. Real-time monitoring would detect this pattern within minutes and auto-block the attacker IP.

userStories.title: Add bot detection and browser fingerprinting for automated attack prevention
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want automated browser-based attacks (like Shannon's headless Chrome exploitation) to be detected and challenged, so that automated pentesting tools cannot complete exploitation flows undetected.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate Cloudflare Turnstile (free CAPTCHA alternative) on: login form, sign-up form, password reset form, contact/feedback forms

Implement browser fingerprinting checks on sensitive actions: detect headless Chrome indicators (navigator.webdriver, missing plugins, automation flags), detect rapid sequential form submissions (>3 submissions in 10 seconds), detect non-human mouse movement patterns on critical forms

Implement progressive challenges: first suspicious request gets Turnstile challenge, second gets rate limited, third gets IP blocked for 1 hour
Log all bot detection events to security_events table from US-099
Ensure accessibility: all CAPTCHA alternatives must be screen-reader accessible
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon uses headless browser automation for exploitation. Bot detection and browser fingerprinting add friction that forces attackers to use more sophisticated (and detectable) evasion techniques.

userStories.title: Remove debug endpoints, development artifacts, and source maps from production builds

Ensure production error boundaries show generic messages (never stack traces, SQL errors, or internal paths)
Verify source maps are NOT deployed to public CDN (only uploaded to Sentry for error tracking)

userStories.title: Implement strict API schema validation on all edge function endpoints
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every edge function to validate request payloads against a strict Zod schema, rejecting any unexpected fields or types, so that type confusion and unexpected input attacks are blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Zod schemas for every edge function's expected input in functions/_shared/schemas/
Every edge function validates request body against its schema as the first operation after auth check

Add maximum string length limits to all text fields (e.g., name: 200 chars, description: 5000 chars, URL: 2048 chars)
Add maximum array length limits to all array fields (prevent resource exhaustion)
Add maximum nesting depth to all object fields (prevent JSON parsing DoS)
Unit tests verify each schema rejects malformed input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Strict schema validation is the most effective input defense. Shannon tests with unexpected types, extra fields, and oversized payloads. Zod schemas reject all of these structurally.

userStories.title: Implement Subresource Integrity (SRI) for all external scripts and stylesheets

acceptanceCriteria: Audit all <script> and <link> tags that load resources from external CDNs
Add integrity= attribute with SHA-384 or SHA-512 hash to each external resource
Add crossorigin='anonymous' attribute alongside SRI attributes
Configure Vite build to generate SRI hashes for all chunk files
Add build-time validation that all external resources have SRI hashes
Document any external resources that cannot use SRI (e.g., dynamic APIs) with risk justification
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: SRI prevents supply chain attacks where a compromised CDN serves malicious scripts. While Shannon doesn't directly test this, it's a defense-in-depth measure against code injection via third-party resources.

userStories.title: Implement IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) protection audit

Verify each endpoint either: relies on RLS policy that filters by auth.uid(), or explicitly checks resource ownership before returning/modifying data
Test each endpoint with valid auth token but another user's resource IDs — all must return 403 or empty result
Audit all Supabase .select() calls in frontend to ensure they include user_id filter (defense in depth beyond RLS)
Add integration tests that create two test users and verify cross-user access is impossible for all resource types
Document IDOR audit findings and fixes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent specifically tests for authorization bypass by manipulating resource identifiers. IDOR is one of the most common and impactful web vulnerabilities.

userStories.title: Implement security regression test suite that runs on every PR

acceptanceCriteria: Create src/test/security/ directory with dedicated security test files

Each test file reproduces the original attack vector and verifies it is blocked
Add npm run test:security:unit that runs only security tests (fast, no external dependencies)
Integrate into GitHub Actions CI — security tests run on every PR alongside regular tests

Add test coverage for every US-085 through US-104 acceptance criterion that can be unit tested

Add width and height attributes to tags in src/pages/Auth.tsx (both Logo-Green.png and Logo-White.png, h-10 = 40px)
Add width and height attributes to tags in src/components/AppSidebar.tsx

userStories.title: Use <picture> elements with WebP sources for logos in Footer, Navigation, Auth, and AppSidebar
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want optimized image formats served so that pages load faster and use less bandwidth (Logo-Green.png is 261KB vs Logo-Green.webp at 28KB).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace bare with in src/components/Footer.tsx
Apply the same <picture> pattern for Logo-White.png/Logo-White.webp in Footer.tsx
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/components/Navigation.tsx for both logos
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/pages/Auth.tsx for both logos

userStories.title: iOS: SwiftUI drag-and-drop to move foods between Pantry, Grocery, and Meal Plan
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want to long-press-drag a food from the Pantry onto a day in the Meal Planner or onto the Grocery tab, so that moving items between areas feels like manipulating physical objects.
acceptanceCriteria: Pantry food rows use .draggable(foodTransferable) with a custom NSItemProvider carrying food id + name
MealPlanView day cells use .dropDestination(for: FoodTransferable.self) to insert a plan_entry for that date/slot
GroceryView root area uses .dropDestination to add the dropped food to grocery_items
TabView tab indicators (Grocery and Planner) accept drops when a drag is hovering, triggering tab switch and drop
Custom drag preview shows food name + category emoji
Haptic feedback on drop accept
Rollback on Supabase failure
Swift builds cleanly and unit tests for FoodTransferable encode/decode added to EatPalTests
userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 1: DONE 2026-04-17 (first pass). Created ios/EatPal/EatPal/Models/FoodTransferable.swift (Codable + Transferable with CodableRepresentation(.utf8PlainText) + ProxyRepresentation for external drag-out of the food name). PantryView FoodRowView has .draggable(FoodTransferable(food:)) with a custom drag preview (category icon + name, shadowed pill). GroceryView.List has .dropDestination(for: FoodTransferable.self) that inserts a GroceryItem with addedVia: "drag" per dropped item. MealPlanView passes date: selectedDate into MealSlotCard. MealSlotCard has .dropDestination that creates a PlanEntry(foodId: dropped.id, date: isoString, mealSlot: slot.rawValue, kidId: activeKidId). isTargeted state drives a green overlay border and a swap-in 'Release to add to X' label. Donates TipEvents.didDragFood on successful drop. Not yet verified on device — TabView-level drop targets (drag-hover-over-tab-to-switch) NOT implemented in this pass; user must navigate to destination tab before dropping.

Low-confidence scans (< 0.5 mean) display a warning banner; per-item confidence < 0.75 displays a questionmark badge.
